title: Periodic Table — Atomic Weights and Physical Properties
source: IUPAC 2021 Standard Atomic Weights, CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics
notes: Atomic weights in parentheses indicate most stable isotope for radioactive elements. Densities at STP unless noted.

elements.name: Hydrogen
elements.usage: Rocket fuel (LH2). Most abundant element in universe. Saturn V S-II and S-IVB stages.

elements.name: Helium
elements.usage: Pressurant gas in rocket tanks. Coolant for IR detectors (JWST). Second most abundant element.

elements.name: Lithium
elements.usage: Batteries (Li-ion). Lightest metal. Spacecraft power systems.

elements.name: Beryllium
elements.usage: JWST primary mirror segments (beryllium). Light, stiff, low thermal expansion at cryogenic temperatures.

elements.name: Boron
elements.usage: Neutron absorber in nuclear shielding. Borosilicate glass (Pyrex) for lab equipment.

elements.name: Carbon
elements.usage: Carbon fiber composites (spacecraft structures). Carbon-carbon for heat shields (Shuttle nose cap). Diamond anvil cells.

elements.name: Nitrogen
elements.usage: Cabin atmosphere component (78% of air). Nitrogen tetroxide (N2O4) oxidizer for hypergolic propulsion.

elements.name: Oxygen
elements.usage: LOX oxidizer (every US launch vehicle). Life support. Pioneer 0's LR-79 burned LOX + RP-1.

elements.name: Fluorine
elements.usage: Fluorine compounds in spacecraft thermal coatings. PTFE (Teflon) insulation.

elements.name: Neon
elements.usage: Ion thruster propellant candidate. Neon signs (cultural significance).

elements.name: Sodium
elements.usage: Sodium vapor in planetary atmospheres (Mercury, Moon exosphere). Telescope guide star lasers.

elements.name: Magnesium
elements.usage: Lightweight alloys (spacecraft structures). Burns brilliantly in oxygen (used in early flash photography).

elements.name: Aluminum
elements.usage: Primary spacecraft structural material. Launch vehicle tanks (Al-Li alloys). Sputnik's polished shell was aluminum.

elements.name: Silicon
elements.usage: Solar cells (crystalline Si). Electronics (every onboard computer). SiO2 thermal protection.

elements.name: Phosphorus
elements.usage: Life support chemistry. Phosphoric acid in fuel cells.

elements.name: Sulfur
elements.usage: Io's volcanic surface (Voyager/Galileo). Sulfuric acid clouds on Venus.

elements.name: Chlorine
elements.usage: Perchlorate oxidizers (solid rocket boosters). Water purification for life support.

elements.name: Argon
elements.usage: Ion thruster propellant. Inert atmosphere for materials processing. Mars atmosphere is 1.9% argon.

elements.name: Potassium
elements.usage: Life support nutrition. Potassium superoxide (KO2) CO2 scrubber in early spacecraft.

elements.name: Calcium
elements.usage: Bone loss in microgravity (ISS research). Calcium spectral lines in stellar classification.

elements.name: Titanium
elements.usage: High-strength, low-weight structural alloys. SR-71 Blackbird (85% titanium). Spacecraft fasteners.

elements.name: Chromium
elements.usage: Chrome steel bearings (Pioneer 0 turbopump). Stainless steel alloys. Corrosion resistance.

elements.name: Manganese
elements.usage: Steel alloy hardener. Manganese nodules on ocean floor (deep sea mining interest).

elements.name: Iron
elements.usage: Steel (spacecraft ground support). Iron meteorites. Mars rust (Fe2O3 gives Mars its red color). Most abundant element by mass in Earth.

elements.name: Cobalt
elements.usage: Superalloys for turbine blades (jet engines, turbopumps). Cobalt-60 radiation calibration sources.

elements.name: Nickel
elements.usage: Inconel superalloys (rocket engine combustion chambers). Nickel-hydrogen batteries (ISS power). Nickel-iron meteorites.

elements.name: Copper
elements.usage: Electrical wiring (all spacecraft). Copper alloy thrust chambers. High thermal conductivity for heat exchangers.

elements.name: Zinc
elements.usage: Galvanic protection. Zinc oxide in thermal control coatings (white paint on spacecraft exteriors).

elements.name: Germanium
elements.usage: Infrared optics (transparent to IR). Germanium detectors for gamma-ray spectroscopy in space.

elements.name: Arsenic
elements.usage: Gallium arsenide (GaAs) solar cells — higher efficiency than silicon, used on Mars rovers and many spacecraft.

elements.name: Krypton
elements.usage: Ion thruster propellant (Starlink satellites use krypton Hall thrusters). Krypton-86 was once the length standard.

elements.name: Zirconium
elements.usage: Thermal barrier coatings. Zirconia ceramics for high-temperature applications.

elements.name: Niobium
elements.usage: Superconducting magnets (particle detectors). Niobium alloy rocket nozzle extensions (RL-10 engine).

elements.name: Molybdenum
elements.usage: High-temperature alloys. Molybdenum disulfide dry lubricant (space mechanisms where liquid lubricants evaporate).

elements.name: Ruthenium
elements.usage: Electrical contacts. Catalyst for spacecraft fuel cells.

elements.name: Palladium
elements.usage: Hydrogen purification membranes. Catalytic converters.

elements.name: Silver
elements.usage: Highest electrical conductivity. Silver-zinc batteries (Apollo). Thermal control coatings (silver-Teflon).

elements.name: Cadmium
elements.usage: Cadmium telluride (CdTe) detectors for X-ray and gamma-ray astronomy (Chandra, NuSTAR).

elements.name: Indium
elements.usage: Indium tin oxide (ITO) transparent conductors. Cryogenic seals (indium wire seals for vacuum systems).

elements.name: Tin
elements.usage: Solder (electronics assembly). Tin whisker problem in spacecraft electronics (short circuits in vacuum).

elements.name: Xenon
elements.usage: Primary ion thruster propellant (Dawn, NEXT-C). High atomic mass gives good specific impulse. Xenon flash lamps.

elements.name: Tungsten
elements.usage: Highest melting point of any element. Rocket nozzle throat inserts. Radiation shielding. Filaments.

elements.name: Osmium
elements.usage: Densest naturally occurring element. Osmium alloy pen tips. Hardness reference.

elements.name: Iridium
elements.usage: Iridium-coated rhenium thrust chambers for long-duration spacecraft engines. K-T boundary iridium layer (asteroid impact marker).

elements.name: Platinum
elements.usage: Catalyst in fuel cells. Thermocouples (Pt-Rh). International prototype kilogram was Pt-Ir alloy.

elements.name: Gold
elements.usage: Thermal control coatings (JWST sunshield). Pioneer 0's gold-plated spacecraft body. Excellent IR reflector. Connector plating.

elements.name: Lead
elements.usage: Radiation shielding (dense, high-Z). Solder (legacy, being replaced by lead-free). Lead-acid batteries (ground support).

elements.name: Bismuth
elements.usage: Bismuth germanate (BGO) scintillation detectors for gamma-ray astronomy.

elements.name: Uranium
elements.usage: Nuclear fission (proposed NERVA nuclear thermal rocket). Depleted uranium radiation shielding. Natural radioactive decay chain.

elements.name: Plutonium
elements.usage: Pu-238 powers RTGs (Radioisotope Thermoelectric Generators). Voyager, Cassini, Curiosity, Perseverance all run on Pu-238 decay heat.
